meaning: adj.两部分的,双重的adv.两部分地,双重地
examples: Her original investment has increased twofold.
The reason for the interview is twofold: we want to find out what he can tell us, plus we also want to find out what condition he is in.
In the most recent quarter, net income rose by more than twofold from the year-earlier period.
The problem for Obama was twofold: His ambitious agenda fed into the GOP narrative.
